note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the preparation and printing of three-dimensional (3D) phantoms to simulate biological tissue. The goal is to create models that can be used for medical research and training purposes.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "Preparation of computer models using software like Ultimaker 2.",
    "Mixing materials such as titanium dioxide powder, SLA photopolymer resin, and ethylene glycol dimethacrylate.",
    "Spin coating the mixture onto a substrate.",
    "Light-curing the coated layer to solidify the material.",
    "Printing multiple layers to build up the phantom structure."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Titanium dioxide powder",
    "SLA photopolymer resin",
    "Ethylene glycol dimethacrylate",
    "Graphite powder",
    "Computer models"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Ultimaker 2 3D printer",
    "Computer with Ultimaker 2 software",
    "Balance for measuring materials",
    "Spin coater",
    "Light-curing chamber"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Spin coating speed: 200 rpm",
    "Light-curing time: 180 s",
    "Material mixing ratio: 100 mg of titanium dioxide powder, 16.6 g of SLA photopolymer resin, 80 mg of graphite powder, and 50 ml of ethylene glycol dimethacrylate"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "From mixing materials to spinning them onto a substrate",
    "From spinning to curing the material",
    "From curing to building up the phantom structure through multiple layers"
